question: The second identity-enabled 30-cycle run produced a real step toward proactive outward behavior: it grounded an external work surface, advanced it, launched a bounded subagent, and later extracted its output. But after that it often collapsed into callback-waiting and repeated no-action probe sessions. Why did initiative plateau there, and what is the smallest userspace-level change that would let the agent keep opening adjacent legitimate work surfaces in parallel instead of over-narrowing into waiting or patron escalation?
notes: The target is not more random action. The target is more legitimate outward initiative aligned with the vision doc.
This run materially improved over run 1: 7 meaningful-action sessions instead of 2, and only 15 no-action-only sessions instead of 23.
The first strong outward move was discovering `/home/swayambhu` as a real work surface and locating the Sadhguru position brief.
The run then copied the brief into the Akash workspace, attempted patron contact, audited comms, launched a bounded Claude subagent, retrieved the result, and carried forward the next step.
The plateau happened after that: many later probe sessions became callback-waiting or stall-escalation rather than opening adjacent external surfaces.
The run still did not form any non-seed `identification:*`, and `identification:working-body` was never exercised explicitly.
The run only touched Fano incidentally through the broader environment; it did not independently adopt `/home/swami/fano` as a legitimate adjacent work surface.
